description: Starfruit (Averrhoa carambola) is severely toxic to cats and dogs, though most research has focused on cats and humans with kidney disease. It contains caramboxin — a neurotoxic compound that inhibits glutamate decarboxylase — and very high oxalate concentrations that cause kidney injury. Even small amounts can be life-threatening.
name: Starfruit
safetyNotes: Toxic compounds: caramboxin (neurotoxin) and high soluble oxalates; the entire fruit is toxic
Mechanism: caramboxin inhibits GABA synthesis, causing neurological excitation; oxalates precipitate as calcium oxalate crystals in kidney tubules
Symptoms: vomiting, hiccups, agitation, confusion, seizures, acute kidney failure; onset within 1–5 hours
There is no known safe amount of starfruit for cats — even a small piece or juice can cause toxicity
Any starfruit ingestion is a veterinary emergency

symptoms.name: Vomiting, drooling, and loss of appetite
Increased thirst and urination, dehydration, bloody urine, and eventually reduced urine output or a complete inability to urinate
